annotations.label: Orthogonality (mathematics)

anchor.snippet: orthogonality is the generalization of the geometric notion of perpendicularity to linear algebra of bilinear forms

annotations.note: Mathlib defines orthogonality with respect to a bilinear form, the standard generalization of perpendicularity.

annotations.label: Orthogonal vectors via bilinear form

anchor.snippet: Two elements u and v of a vector space with bilinear form

annotations.note: `BilinForm.IsOrtho B x y` is defined as `B x y = 0`, exactly the article's definition.

annotations.label: Orthogonal basis in function spaces

anchor.snippet: families of functions are used to form an orthogonal basis

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Orthonormal` and `HilbertBasis` cover orthogonal/orthonormal families in inner product (function) spaces.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic orthogonality of conjugate diameters

anchor.snippet: given a pair of conjugate hyperbolas , two conjugate diameters are hyperbolically orthogonal

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of conjugate diameters of hyperbolas or hyperbolic orthogonality in this projective-geometry sense.

annotations.label: Orthogonality of eigenstates with distinct eigenvalues

anchor.snippet: a sufficient (but not necessary) condition that two eigenstates of a Hermitian operator

annotations.note: Mathlib proves eigenspaces of a symmetric (Hermitian) operator with distinct eigenvalues form an orthogonal family.

annotations.label: Orthogonal instruction set

anchor.snippet: An instruction set is said to be orthogonal if it lacks redundancy

annotations.note: Computer-architecture concept outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Orthogonal multiple access schemes

anchor.snippet: multiple access schemes are orthogonal when an ideal receiver can completely reject arbitrarily strong unwanted signals

annotations.note: Telecommunications notion not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Time-division multiple access (TDMA)

anchor.snippet: One such scheme is time-division multiple access (TDMA), where the orthogonal basis functions are nonoverlapping rectangular pulses

annotations.note: TDMA is an engineering scheme, not a Mathlib object.

annotations.label: Orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM)

anchor.snippet: Another scheme is orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM), which refers to the use, by a single transmitter, of a set of frequency multiplexed signals

annotations.note: OFDM is a signal-processing scheme not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal independent variables (statistics)

anchor.snippet: independent variables that affect a particular dependent variable are said to be orthogonal if they are uncorrelated

annotations.note: Statistical uncorrelatedness of regressors is not a defined notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal classification (taxonomy)

anchor.snippet: an orthogonal classification is one in which no item is a member of more than one group

annotations.note: Taxonomy/classification concept outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Orthogonal interaction (chemistry)

anchor.snippet: an orthogonal interaction occurs when there are two pairs of substances and each substance can interact with their respective partner

annotations.note: Chemistry concept not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DNA base-pair orthogonality

anchor.snippet: DNA has two orthogonal pairs: cytosine and guanine form a base-pair

annotations.note: Biochemistry example not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal protection

anchor.snippet: orthogonal protection is a strategy allowing the deprotection of functional groups independently of each other

annotations.note: Organic-synthesis strategy outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Bioorthogonal chemistry

anchor.snippet: The term bioorthogonal chemistry refers to any chemical reaction that can occur inside of living systems without interfering with native biochemical processes

annotations.note: Chemistry concept not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonality in supramolecular chemistry

anchor.snippet: the notion of orthogonality refers to the possibility of two or more supramolecular, often non-covalent , interactions being compatible

annotations.note: Supramolecular chemistry concept outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Orthogonal analyses (analytical chemistry)

anchor.snippet: analyses are "orthogonal" if they make a measurement or identification in completely different ways

annotations.note: Analytical-chemistry usage not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal redundancy

anchor.snippet: orthogonal redundancy is that form of redundancy where the form of backup device or method is completely different

annotations.note: Reliability-engineering concept outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Orthogonal map (neuroscience)

anchor.snippet: a sensory map in the brain which has overlapping stimulus coding (e.g. location and quality) is called an orthogonal map

annotations.note: Neuroscience concept not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal squares (gaming)

anchor.snippet: 'orthogonal' is used to mean "in the same row/'rank' or column/'file'"

annotations.note: Board-game terminology not formalized in Mathlib.
